commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

commit.message:
Fix signal sync overwriting user-edited positions

syncCopiedPositions was reverting user edits because positions
created before the edit feature had no lastEditedAt field.

Now uses signalFieldsSnapshot: only overwrites a field if it
still matches the original signal value (user hasn't changed it).
New positions from signals store the snapshot at creation time.

fix(login): auto-redirect to login when password already set

When a user tries to set a password via the first-login form but their
password is already defined, the backend now returns a specific
PASSWORD_ALREADY_SET code instead of the generic SETUP_FAILED.

The frontend catches this code and automatically returns to the login
screen (keeping the email pre-filled) with a clear message:
"Votre mot de passe est déjà défini. Connectez-vous avec vos identifiants."

No more manual "Retour à la connexion" click required.

commit.message:
feat: blank target word in example sentence until correctly typed

The target word is replaced with dashed underscores (___) in the
example sentence from the moment the word loads. When the user
correctly completes the word, the blank is filled with the actual
word via a spring-bounce reveal animation in white + cyan glow.
On skip, the word is revealed in rose/red to mark it as missed.

Works in both normal mode (sentence below Turkish) and exam mode
(sentence is the main prompt in the big area).

commit.message:
fix(sp): recrear formaPago como multi-line para soportar JSON completo del plan de pago

CAUSA RAÍZ CONFIRMADA via auditoría en Chrome:
- formaPago en CRM_Cierres y CRM_Clientes era single-line text (máx 255 chars)
- El JSON completo del plan de pago (banco, cuenta, nCuotas, calendario[])
  supera los 255 chars → SharePoint devuelve 400 Bad Request en cada save
- spCreate/spUpdate caían al fallback de 'campos mínimos'

FIX:
1. spAsegurarColumnas ahora ELIMINA formaPago (single-line) antes del loop
   y lo RECREA como text{allowMultipleLines:true} (multi-line, hasta 63999 chars)
2. colsDef actualizado: formaPago → allowMultipleLines:true en CRM_Cierres y CRM_Clientes
3. Bump sp_cols_done '11'→'12' para forzar re-setup en todos los usuarios

commit.message:
Add organization structure with multi-tenant support (#19)

Users now belong to organizations. On signup, each user automatically gets a personal organization. Users can be invited to other organizations and belong to multiple orgs. The UI includes an org switcher to change active workspace.

Database: 3 new tables (organizations, profiles, organization_members) with RLS policies to enforce org-level isolation. Auth callback ensures profiles exist for all users. Helper functions prevent RLS recursion in self-referential policies.

App: Profile fetching, org membership queries, org switcher component in sidebar, and an API endpoint to switch active org.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
security: API 보호 + Binance 레이트리밋 + CSP 헤더 적용

1. API 쓰기 보호
   - PATCH /api/settings: ADMIN_SECRET 헤더 인증 필수
   - 허용된 키만 처리, 값 범위 서버 검증(clamp)
   - 설정 페이지 UI에 Admin Key 입력란 추가 (localStorage 저장)

2. Binance 레이트리밋 대응
   - 429/418 즉시 에러 + Retry-After 로깅
   - X-MBX-USED-WEIGHT-1M > 960 시 경고 로그

3. CSP / 보안 헤더 (next.config.ts)
   - X-Frame-Options: DENY (클릭재킹 방지)
   - X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
   - Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
   - Permissions-Policy: 카메라/마이크/위치 차단
   - Content-Security-Policy: 허용 출처 명시

commit.message:
fix(email): E3.2 trust-critical email guardrails — noise is hard to earn (#E3.2) (#571)

Pure deterministic guardrails module (no LLM, no DB, no side effects):
- Business inquiry keywords override any noise/ignore classification
- Forwarded email defaults to review, never auto-ignored
- Noise requires strong evidence (multiple signals: promo+no-reply or promo+unsubscribe)

Wired into all 3 triage paths:
- postSyncPipeline.ts: guardrails applied before triage persistence
- email_triage.ts job handler: guardrails wrapped around persistence loop
- executiveFrame.ts: guardFrameAction() applied after LLM normalization

UI language softened:
- "Safe to ignore" → "Likely low priority"
- "Ignore" → "Low priority"

Co-authored-by: Matt <mpaller@yahoo.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix silent failure when TileConfiguration.txt is missing

Three bugs caused the acquisition to fail silently from the user's
perspective when tile positions were not found:

1. set_state("FAILED") called without error message, so Java client
   never received a failure reason
2. starting_position referenced in finally block but assigned after
   tile loading -- moved assignment before tile config read
3. error_msg[:50] in handle_client crashed on None when error message
   was not set, killing the error reporting path entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(go): improve DateTime deserialization with multi-format fallback (#13131)

* feat(go): improve DateTime deserialization with multi-format fallback

Update DateTime.UnmarshalJSON to handle multiple timestamp formats:
- RFC3339Nano (superset of RFC3339, supports fractional seconds)
- ISO 8601 without timezone (assumes UTC)
- Date-only format (assumes UTC)
- Unix epoch seconds (integer)

This matches the flexibility of Java, Python, TypeScript, and C# generators
and prevents deserialization failures when APIs return timestamps in different
ISO 8601 variants.

Co-Authored-By: judah <jsklan.development@gmail.com>

* fix(go): improve DateTime deserialization error reporting

Collect and include all parsing errors when DateTime.UnmarshalJSON fails
instead of returning a generic error message. This provides detailed
information about why each format (RFC3339Nano, ISO8601, date-only) failed
to parse, making debugging datetime parsing issues much easier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update time.go

* chore: update seed snapshots for go-model and go-sdk with new DateTime deserialization

Co-Authored-By: judah <jsklan.development@gmail.com>

Fix save search UX and pipeline realtime reliability

Save search: Replace transient banner with persistent "Save search"
button next to results count. The old banner only appeared after
fresh searches — cached results from localStorage never showed it.
Now the button is always visible when results are present.

Pipeline realtime: Listen to all events (INSERT + UPDATE) instead
of just UPDATE. Add subscription status handling with console warning
on failure. Add 10-second polling fallback if realtime connection
fails or times out, auto-cleared when realtime connects.
